title: Warehouse Inventory Light-Based Shelf Monitoring
app: Splunk Edge Hub (light sensor array), custom container for pattern recognition
spl:
| mstats avg(light_level) as lux by host, rack_id, shelf_position, _time span=5m
| delta lux as lux_change
| eval significant_change=if(abs(lux_change) > 20, "YES", "NO")
| stats count(eval(significant_change="YES")) as change_events, avg(lux) as avg_lux by host, rack_id, shelf_position
| where change_events > 5
| eval inventory_status=case(avg_lux > 1000, "EMPTY", avg_lux > 500, "LOW", 1=1, "STOCKED")
description: Detects empty or partially depleted shelves in real-time by monitoring light pattern changes in high-bay storage.
value: Detects empty or partially depleted shelves in real-time by monitoring light pattern changes in high-bay storage.
grandmaExplanation: We catch empty or partially depleted shelves in real-time by monitoring light pattern changes in high-bay storage — so faults, tampering, and safety issues are spotted right away.
implementation: Mount Edge Hub light sensor facing shelving unit. Deploy custom Python container that learns baseline light patterns for each shelf over 1-week baseline period. Use machine learning to detect sustained light increases (empty shelf) vs brief shadows (restocking activity). Reference Advanced Settings → Containers tab to set container polling interval to 5 minutes. Store 3M light data points locally for historical baseline calculation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (light sensor array), custom container for pattern recognition.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=light, `sourcetype=edge_hub`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Mount Edge Hub light sensor facing shelving unit. Deploy custom Python container that learns baseline light patterns for each shelf over 1-week baseline period. Use machine learning to detect sustained light increases (empty shelf) vs brief shadows (restocking activity). Reference Advanced Settings → Containers tab to set container polling interval to 5 minutes. Store 3M light data points locally for historical baseline calculation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats avg(light_level) as lux by host, rack_id, shelf_position, _time span=5m
| delta lux as lux_change
| eval significant_change=if(abs(lux_change) > 20, "YES", "NO")
| stats count(eval(significant_change="YES")) as change_events, avg(lux) as avg_lux by host, rack_id, shelf_position
| where change_events > 5
| eval inventory_status=case(avg_lux > 1000, "EMPTY", avg_lux > 500, "LOW", 1=1, "STOCKED")
```

Understanding this SPL

**Warehouse Inventory Light-Based Shelf Monitoring** — Detects empty or partially depleted shelves in real-time by monitoring light pattern changes in high-bay storage.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=light, `sourcetype=edge_hub`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (light sensor array), custom container for pattern recognition. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).
• Pipeline stage (see **Warehouse Inventory Light-Based Shelf Monitoring**): delta lux as lux_change
• `eval` defines or adjusts **significant_change** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, rack_id, shelf_position** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where change_events > 5` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **inventory_status** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Shelf occupancy heatmap, light level trend by shelf, inventory status dashboard.
visualization: Shelf occupancy heatmap, light level trend by shelf, inventory status dashboard.
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
